   14571                 :            : 
   14572                 :            : /* The intent behind this definition is that it'll catch
   14573                 :            :  * any uses of REJECT which flex missed.
   14574                 :            :  */
   14575                 :            : #define REJECT reject_used_but_not_detected
   14576                 :            : #define yymore() yymore_used_but_not_detected
   14577                 :            : #define YY_MORE_ADJ 0

   14580                 :            : #line 1 "scan.l"
   14581                 :            : #line 2 "scan.l"
   14582                 :            : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
   14583                 :            :  *
   14584                 :            :  * scan.l
   14585                 :            :  *        lexical scanner for PostgreSQL
   14586                 :            :  *
   14587                 :            :  * NOTE NOTE NOTE:
   14588                 :            :  *
   14589                 :            :  * The rules in this file must be kept in sync with psql's lexer!!!
   14590                 :            :  *
   14591                 :            :  * The rules are designed so that the scanner never has to backtrack,
   14592                 :            :  * in the sense that there is always a rule that can match the input
   14593                 :            :  * consumed so far (the rule action may internally throw back some input
   14594                 :            :  * with yyless(), however).  As explained in the flex manual, this makes
   14595                 :            :  * for a useful speed increase --- about a third faster than a plain -CF
   14596                 :            :  * lexer, in simple testing.  The extra complexity is mostly in the rules
   14597                 :            :  * for handling float numbers and continued string literals.  If you change
   14598                 :            :  * the lexical rules, verify that you haven't broken the no-backtrack
   14599                 :            :  * property by running flex with the "-b" option and checking that the
   14600                 :            :  * resulting "lex.backup" file says that no backing up is needed.
   14601                 :            :  *
   14602                 :            :  *
   14603                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2010, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
   14604                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
   14605                 :            :  *
   14606                 :            :  * IDENTIFICATION
   14607                 :            :  *        $PostgreSQL: pgsql/src/backend/parser/scan.l,v 1.168 2010/07/20 00:34:44 rhaas Exp $
   14608                 :            :  *
   14609                 :            :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
   14610                 :            :  */
   14611                 :            : #include "postgres.h"
   14612                 :            : 
   14613                 :            : #include <ctype.h>
   14614                 :            : #include <unistd.h>
   14615                 :            : 
   14616                 :            : #include "parser/gramparse.h"
   14617                 :            : #include "parser/keywords.h"
   14618                 :            : /* Not needed now that this file is compiled as part of gram.y */
   14619                 :            : /* #include "parser/gram.h" */
   14620                 :            : #include "parser/scansup.h"
   14621                 :            : #include "mb/pg_wchar.h"
   14622                 :            : 
   14623                 :            : #define unify_version(a,b,c) ((a<<16)+(b<<8)+c)
   14624                 :            : #if unify_version(YY_FLEX_MAJOR_VERSION,YY_FLEX_MINOR_VERSION,YY_FLEX_SUBMINOR_VERSION) < unify_version(2,5,35)
   14625                 :            : int base_yyget_lineno  (void);

   14627                 :            : FILE *base_yyget_out  (void);

   14630                 :            : void base_yyset_lineno (int  line_number );

   14638                 :            : 
   14639                 :            : /* Avoid exit() on fatal scanner errors (a bit ugly -- see yy_fatal_error) */
   14640                 :            : #undef fprintf
   14641                 :            : #define fprintf(file, fmt, msg)  fprintf_to_ereport(fmt, msg)
   14642                 :            : 
   14643                 :            : static void
   14644                 :            : fprintf_to_ereport(const char *fmt, const char *msg)
   14645                 :            : {
   14646                 :            :         ereport(ERROR, (errmsg_internal("%s", msg)));
   14647                 :            : }
   14648                 :            : 
   14649                 :            : static int              xcdepth = 0;    /* depth of nesting in slash-star comments */
   14650                 :            : static char    *dolqstart;      /* current $foo$ quote start string */
   14651                 :            : 
   14652                 :            : /* first part of UTF16 surrogate for unicode escapes */
   14653                 :            : static uint32   utf16_top_part;
   14654                 :            : 
   14655                 :            : 
   14656                 :            : /*
   14657                 :            :  * GUC variables.  This is a DIRECT violation of the warning given at the
   14658                 :            :  * head of gram.y, ie flex/bison code must not depend on any GUC variables;
   14659                 :            :  * as such, changing their values can induce very unintuitive behavior.
   14660                 :            :  * But we shall have to live with it as a short-term thing until the switch
   14661                 :            :  * to SQL-standard string syntax is complete.
   14662                 :            :  */
   14663                 :            : int                             backslash_quote = BACKSLASH_QUOTE_SAFE_ENCODING;
   14664                 :            : bool                    escape_string_warning = true;
   14665                 :            : bool                    standard_conforming_strings = true;
   14666                 :            : 
   14667                 :            : static bool             warn_on_first_escape;
   14668                 :            : static bool             saw_non_ascii = false;
   14669                 :            : 
   14670                 :            : /*
   14671                 :            :  * literalbuf is used to accumulate literal values when multiple rules
   14672                 :            :  * are needed to parse a single literal.  Call startlit to reset buffer
   14673                 :            :  * to empty, addlit to add text.  Note that the buffer is palloc'd and
   14674                 :            :  * starts life afresh on every parse cycle.
   14675                 :            :  */
   14676                 :            : static char        *literalbuf;         /* expandable buffer */
   14677                 :            : static int              literallen;             /* actual current length */
   14678                 :            : static int              literalalloc;   /* current allocated buffer size */
   14679                 :            : 
   14680                 :            : #define startlit()  (literalbuf[0] = '\0', literallen = 0)
   14681                 :            : static void addlit(char *ytext, int yleng);
   14682                 :            : static void addlitchar(unsigned char ychar);
   14683                 :            : static char *litbufdup(void);
   14684                 :            : static char *litbuf_udeescape(unsigned char escape);
   14685                 :            : 
   14686                 :            : #define lexer_errposition()  scanner_errposition(yylloc)
   14687                 :            : 
   14688                 :            : static void check_escape_warning(void);
   14689                 :            : static void check_string_escape_warning(unsigned char ychar);
   14690                 :            : 
   14691                 :            : /*
   14692                 :            :  * Each call to base_yylex must set yylloc to the location of the found token
   14693                 :            :  * (expressed as a byte offset from the start of the input text).
   14694                 :            :  * When we parse a token that requires multiple lexer rules to process,
   14695                 :            :  * this should be done in the first such rule, else yylloc will point
   14696                 :            :  * into the middle of the token.
   14697                 :            :  */
   14698                 :            : #define SET_YYLLOC()  (yylloc = base_yytext - scanbuf)
   14699                 :            : 
   14700                 :            : /* Handles to the buffer that the lexer uses internally */
   14701                 :            : static YY_BUFFER_STATE scanbufhandle;
   14702                 :            : static char *scanbuf;
   14703                 :            : 
   14704                 :            : static unsigned char unescape_single_char(unsigned char c);
   14705                 :            : static void addunicode(pg_wchar c);
   14706                 :            : 
   14707                 :            : #define YY_NO_INPUT 1
   14708                 :            : /*
   14709                 :            :  * OK, here is a short description of lex/flex rules behavior.
   14710                 :            :  * The longest pattern which matches an input string is always chosen.
   14711                 :            :  * For equal-length patterns, the first occurring in the rules list is chosen.
   14712                 :            :  * INITIAL is the starting state, to which all non-conditional rules apply.
   14713                 :            :  * Exclusive states change parsing rules while the state is active.  When in
   14714                 :            :  * an exclusive state, only those rules defined for that state apply.
   14715                 :            :  *
   14716                 :            :  * We use exclusive states for quoted strings, extended comments,
   14717                 :            :  * and to eliminate parsing troubles for numeric strings.
   14718                 :            :  * Exclusive states:
   14719                 :            :  *  <xb> bit string literal
   14720                 :            :  *  <xc> extended C-style comments
   14721                 :            :  *  <xd> delimited identifiers (double-quoted identifiers)
   14722                 :            :  *  <xh> hexadecimal numeric string
   14723                 :            :  *  <xq> standard quoted strings
   14724                 :            :  *  <xe> extended quoted strings (support backslash escape sequences)
   14725                 :            :  *  <xdolq> $foo$ quoted strings
   14726                 :            :  *  <xui> quoted identifier with Unicode escapes
   14727                 :            :  *  <xus> quoted string with Unicode escapes
   14728                 :            :  *  <xeu> Unicode surrogate escape in extended string
   14729                 :            :  */
   14730                 :            : 
   14731                 :            : 
   14732                 :            : 
   14733                 :            : 
   14734                 :            : 
   14735                 :            : 
   14736                 :            : 
   14737                 :            : 
   14738                 :            : 
   14739                 :            : 
   14740                 :            : /*
   14741                 :            :  * In order to make the world safe for Windows and Mac clients as well as
   14742                 :            :  * Unix ones, we accept either \n or \r as a newline.  A DOS-style \r\n
   14743                 :            :  * sequence will be seen as two successive newlines, but that doesn't cause
   14744                 :            :  * any problems.  Comments that start with -- and extend to the next
   14745                 :            :  * newline are treated as equivalent to a single whitespace character.
   14746                 :            :  *
   14747                 :            :  * NOTE a fine point: if there is no newline following --, we will absorb
   14748                 :            :  * everything to the end of the input as a comment.  This is correct.  Older
   14749                 :            :  * versions of Postgres failed to recognize -- as a comment if the input
   14750                 :            :  * did not end with a newline.
   14751                 :            :  *
   14752                 :            :  * XXX perhaps \f (formfeed) should be treated as a newline as well?
   14753                 :            :  *
   14754                 :            :  * XXX if you change the set of whitespace characters, fix scanner_isspace()
   14755                 :            :  * to agree, and see also the plpgsql lexer.
   14756                 :            :  */
   14757                 :            : /*
   14758                 :            :  * SQL requires at least one newline in the whitespace separating
   14759                 :            :  * string literals that are to be concatenated.  Silly, but who are we
   14760                 :            :  * to argue?  Note that {whitespace_with_newline} should not have * after
   14761                 :            :  * it, whereas {whitespace} should generally have a * after it...
   14762                 :            :  */
   14763                 :            : /*
   14764                 :            :  * To ensure that {quotecontinue} can be scanned without having to back up
   14765                 :            :  * if the full pattern isn't matched, we include trailing whitespace in
   14766                 :            :  * {quotestop}.  This matches all cases where {quotecontinue} fails to match,
   14767                 :            :  * except for {quote} followed by whitespace and just one "-" (not two,
   14768                 :            :  * which would start a {comment}).  To cover that we have {quotefail}.
   14769                 :            :  * The actions for {quotestop} and {quotefail} must throw back characters
   14770                 :            :  * beyond the quote proper.
   14771                 :            :  */
   14772                 :            : /* Bit string
   14773                 :            :  * It is tempting to scan the string for only those characters
   14774                 :            :  * which are allowed. However, this leads to silently swallowed
   14775                 :            :  * characters if illegal characters are included in the string.
   14776                 :            :  * For example, if xbinside is [01] then B'ABCD' is interpreted
   14777                 :            :  * as a zero-length string, and the ABCD' is lost!
   14778                 :            :  * Better to pass the string forward and let the input routines
   14779                 :            :  * validate the contents.
   14780                 :            :  */
   14781                 :            : /* Hexadecimal number */
   14782                 :            : /* National character */
   14783                 :            : /* Quoted string that allows backslash escapes */
   14784                 :            : /* Extended quote
   14785                 :            :  * xqdouble implements embedded quote, ''''
   14786                 :            :  */
   14787                 :            : /* $foo$ style quotes ("dollar quoting")
   14788                 :            :  * The quoted string starts with $foo$ where "foo" is an optional string
   14789                 :            :  * in the form of an identifier, except that it may not contain "$", 
   14790                 :            :  * and extends to the first occurrence of an identical string.  
   14791                 :            :  * There is *no* processing of the quoted text.
   14792                 :            :  *
   14793                 :            :  * {dolqfailed} is an error rule to avoid scanner backup when {dolqdelim}
   14794                 :            :  * fails to match its trailing "$".
   14795                 :            :  */
   14796                 :            : /* Double quote
   14797                 :            :  * Allows embedded spaces and other special characters into identifiers.
   14798                 :            :  */
   14799                 :            : /* Unicode escapes */
   14800                 :            : /* error rule to avoid backup */
   14801                 :            : /* Quoted identifier with Unicode escapes */
   14802                 :            : /* Quoted string with Unicode escapes */
   14803                 :            : /* error rule to avoid backup */
   14804                 :            : /* C-style comments
   14805                 :            :  *
   14806                 :            :  * The "extended comment" syntax closely resembles allowable operator syntax.
   14807                 :            :  * The tricky part here is to get lex to recognize a string starting with
   14808                 :            :  * slash-star as a comment, when interpreting it as an operator would produce
   14809                 :            :  * a longer match --- remember lex will prefer a longer match!  Also, if we
   14810                 :            :  * have something like plus-slash-star, lex will think this is a 3-character
   14811                 :            :  * operator whereas we want to see it as a + operator and a comment start.
   14812                 :            :  * The solution is two-fold:
   14813                 :            :  * 1. append {op_chars}* to xcstart so that it matches as much text as
   14814                 :            :  *    {operator} would. Then the tie-breaker (first matching rule of same
   14815                 :            :  *    length) ensures xcstart wins.  We put back the extra stuff with yyless()
   14816                 :            :  *    in case it contains a star-slash that should terminate the comment.
   14817                 :            :  * 2. In the operator rule, check for slash-star within the operator, and
   14818                 :            :  *    if found throw it back with yyless().  This handles the plus-slash-star
   14819                 :            :  *    problem.
   14820                 :            :  * Dash-dash comments have similar interactions with the operator rule.
   14821                 :            :  */
   14822                 :            : /*
   14823                 :            :  * "self" is the set of chars that should be returned as single-character
   14824                 :            :  * tokens.  "op_chars" is the set of chars that can make up "Op" tokens,
   14825                 :            :  * which can be one or more characters long (but if a single-char token
   14826                 :            :  * appears in the "self" set, it is not to be returned as an Op).  Note
   14827                 :            :  * that the sets overlap, but each has some chars that are not in the other.
   14828                 :            :  *
   14829                 :            :  * If you change either set, adjust the character lists appearing in the
   14830                 :            :  * rule for "operator"!
   14831                 :            :  */
   14832                 :            : /* we no longer allow unary minus in numbers. 
   14833                 :            :  * instead we pass it separately to parser. there it gets
   14834                 :            :  * coerced via doNegate() -- Leon aug 20 1999
   14835                 :            :  *
   14836                 :            :  * {realfail1} and {realfail2} are added to prevent the need for scanner
   14837                 :            :  * backup when the {real} rule fails to match completely.
   14838                 :            :  */
   14839                 :            : /*
   14840                 :            :  * Dollar quoted strings are totally opaque, and no escaping is done on them.
   14841                 :            :  * Other quoted strings must allow some special characters such as single-quote
   14842                 :            :  *  and newline.
   14843                 :            :  * Embedded single-quotes are implemented both in the SQL standard
   14844                 :            :  *  style of two adjacent single quotes "''" and in the Postgres/Java style
   14845                 :            :  *  of escaped-quote "\'".
   14846                 :            :  * Other embedded escaped characters are matched explicitly and the leading
   14847                 :            :  *  backslash is dropped from the string.
   14848                 :            :  * Note that xcstart must appear before operator, as explained above!
   14849                 :            :  *  Also whitespace (comment) must appear before operator.
   14850                 :            :  */
   14851                 :            : #line 14852 "scan.c"
   14852                 :            : 
   14853                 :            : #define INITIAL 0
   14854                 :            : #define xb 1
   14855                 :            : #define xc 2
   14856                 :            : #define xd 3
   14857                 :            : #define xh 4
   14858                 :            : #define xe 5
   14859                 :            : #define xq 6
   14860                 :            : #define xdolq 7
   14861                 :            : #define xui 8
   14862                 :            : #define xus 9
   14863                 :            : #define xeu 10
   14864                 :            : 
   14865                 :            : #ifndef YY_NO_UNISTD_H
   14866                 :            : /* Special case for "unistd.h", since it is non-ANSI. We include it way
   14867                 :            :  * down here because we want the user's section 1 to have been scanned first.
   14868                 :            :  * The user has a chance to override it with an option.
   14869                 :            :  */
   14870                 :            : #include <unistd.h>
   14871                 :            : #endif
   14872                 :            : 
   14873                 :            : #ifndef YY_EXTRA_TYPE
   14874                 :            : #define YY_EXTRA_TYPE void *
   14875                 :            : #endif
   14876                 :            : 
   14877                 :            : static int yy_init_globals (void );
   14878                 :            : 
   14879                 :            : /* Accessor methods to globals.
   14880                 :            :    These are made visible to non-reentrant scanners for convenience. */
   14881                 :            : 
   14882                 :            : int base_yylex_destroy (void );
   14883                 :            : 
   14884                 :            : int base_yyget_debug (void );
   14885                 :            : 
   14886                 :            : void base_yyset_debug (int debug_flag  );
   14887                 :            : 
   14888                 :            : YY_EXTRA_TYPE base_yyget_extra (void );
   14889                 :            : 
   14890                 :            : void base_yyset_extra (YY_EXTRA_TYPE user_defined  );

   14942                 :            : 
   14943                 :            : /* Copy whatever the last rule matched to the standard output. */

   14950                 :            : 
   14951                 :            : /* Gets input and stuffs it into "buf".  number of characters read, or YY_NULL,
   14952                 :            :  * is returned in "result".
   14953                 :            :  */
   14954                 :            : #ifndef YY_INPUT
   14955                 :            : #define YY_INPUT(buf,result,max_size) \
   14956                 :            :         if ( YY_CURRENT_BUFFER_LVALUE->yy_is_interactive ) \
   14957                 :            :                 { \
   14958                 :            :                 int c = '*'; \
   14959                 :            :                 yy_size_t n; \
   14960                 :            :                 for ( n = 0; n < max_size && \
   14961                 :            :                              (c = getc( base_yyin )) != EOF && c != '\n'; ++n ) \
   14962                 :            :                         buf[n] = (char) c; \
   14963                 :            :                 if ( c == '\n' ) \
   14964                 :            :                         buf[n++] = (char) c; \
   14965                 :            :                 if ( c == EOF && ferror( base_yyin ) ) \
   14966                 :            :                         YY_FATAL_ERROR( "input in flex scanner failed" ); \
   14967                 :            :                 result = n; \
   14968                 :            :                 } \
   14969                 :            :         else \
   14970                 :            :                 { \
   14971                 :            :                 errno=0; \
   14972                 :            :                 while ( (result = fread(buf, 1, max_size, base_yyin))==0 && ferror(base_yyin)) \
   14973                 :            :                         { \
   14974                 :            :                         if( errno != EINTR) \
   14975                 :            :                                 { \
   14976                 :            :                                 YY_FATAL_ERROR( "input in flex scanner failed" ); \
   14977                 :            :                                 break; \
   14978                 :            :                                 } \
   14979                 :            :                         errno=0; \
   14980                 :            :                         clearerr(base_yyin); \
   14981                 :            :                         } \
   14982                 :            :                 }\
   14983                 :            : \
   14984                 :            : 
   14985                 :            : #endif

   16880                 :            : /*
   16881                 :            :  * scanner_errposition
   16882                 :            :  *              Report a lexer or grammar error cursor position, if possible.
   16883                 :            :  *
   16884                 :            :  * This is expected to be used within an ereport() call.  The return value
   16885                 :            :  * is a dummy (always 0, in fact).
   16886                 :            :  *
   16887                 :            :  * Note that this can only be used for messages emitted during raw parsing
   16888                 :            :  * (essentially, scan.l and gram.y), since it requires scanbuf to still be
   16889                 :            :  * valid.
   16890                 :            :  */
   16891                 :            : int
   16892                 :            : scanner_errposition(int location)
   16893                 :            : {
   16894                 :            :         int             pos;
   16895                 :            : 
   16896                 :            :         Assert(scanbuf != NULL);        /* else called from wrong place */
   16897                 :            :         if (location < 0)
   16898                 :            :                 return 0;                               /* no-op if location is unknown */
   16899                 :            : 
   16900                 :            :         /* Convert byte offset to character number */
   16901                 :            :         pos = pg_mbstrlen_with_len(scanbuf, location) + 1;
   16902                 :            :         /* And pass it to the ereport mechanism */
   16903                 :            :         return errposition(pos);
   16904                 :            : }
   16905                 :            : 
   16906                 :            : /*
   16907                 :            :  * yyerror
   16908                 :            :  *              Report a lexer or grammar error.
   16909                 :            :  *
   16910                 :            :  * The message's cursor position identifies the most recently lexed token.
   16911                 :            :  * This is OK for syntax error messages from the Bison parser, because Bison
   16912                 :            :  * parsers report error as soon as the first unparsable token is reached.
   16913                 :            :  * Beware of using yyerror for other purposes, as the cursor position might
   16914                 :            :  * be misleading!
   16915                 :            :  */
   16916                 :            : void
   16917                 :            : yyerror(const char *message)
   16918                 :            : {
   16919                 :            :         const char *loc = scanbuf + yylloc;
   16920                 :            : 
   16921                 :            :         if (*loc == YY_END_OF_BUFFER_CHAR)
   16922                 :            :         {
   16923                 :            :                 ereport(ERROR,
   16924                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   16925                 :            :                                  /* translator: %s is typically the translation of "syntax error" */
   16926                 :            :                                  errmsg("%s at end of input", _(message)),
   16927                 :            :                                  lexer_errposition()));
   16928                 :            :         }
   16929                 :            :         else
   16930                 :            :         {
   16931                 :            :                 ereport(ERROR,
   16932                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   16933                 :            :                                  /* translator: first %s is typically the translation of "syntax error" */
   16934                 :            :                                  errmsg("%s at or near \"%s\"", _(message), loc),
   16935                 :            :                                  lexer_errposition()));
   16936                 :            :         }
   16937                 :            : }

   16943                 :            : void
   16944                 :            : scanner_init(const char *str)
   16945                 :            : {
   16946                 :            :         Size    slen = strlen(str);
   16947                 :            : 
   16948                 :            :         /*
   16949                 :            :          * Might be left over after ereport()

   16954                 :            :         /*
   16955                 :            :          * Make a scan buffer with special termination needed by flex.
   16956                 :            :          */
   16957                 :            :         scanbuf = palloc(slen + 2);
   16958                 :            :         memcpy(scanbuf, str, slen);
   16959                 :            :         scanbuf[slen] = scanbuf[slen + 1] = YY_END_OF_BUFFER_CHAR;
   16960                 :            :         scanbufhandle = base_yy_scan_buffer(scanbuf,slen + 2);

   16962                 :            :         /* initialize literal buffer to a reasonable but expansible size */
   16963                 :            :         literalalloc = 1024;
   16964                 :            :         literalbuf = (char *) palloc(literalalloc);
   16965                 :            :         startlit();

   16974                 :            : void
   16975                 :            : scanner_finish(void)

   16983                 :            : static void
   16984                 :            : addlit(char *ytext, int yleng)
   16985                 :            : {
   16986                 :            :         /* enlarge buffer if needed */
   16987                 :            :         if ((literallen+yleng) >= literalalloc)
   16988                 :            :         {
   16989                 :            :                 do {
   16990                 :            :                         literalalloc *= 2;
   16991                 :            :                 } while ((literallen+yleng) >= literalalloc);
   16992                 :            :                 literalbuf = (char *) repalloc(literalbuf, literalalloc);
   16993                 :            :         }
   16994                 :            :         /* append new data, add trailing null */
   16995                 :            :         memcpy(literalbuf+literallen, ytext, yleng);
   16996                 :            :         literallen += yleng;
   16997                 :            :         literalbuf[literallen] = '\0';
   16998                 :            : }
   16999                 :            : 
   17000                 :            : 
   17001                 :            : static void
   17002                 :            : addlitchar(unsigned char ychar)
   17003                 :            : {
   17004                 :            :         /* enlarge buffer if needed */
   17005                 :            :         if ((literallen+1) >= literalalloc)
   17006                 :            :         {
   17007                 :            :                 literalalloc *= 2;
   17008                 :            :                 literalbuf = (char *) repalloc(literalbuf, literalalloc);
   17009                 :            :         }
   17010                 :            :         /* append new data, add trailing null */
   17011                 :            :         literalbuf[literallen] = ychar;
   17012                 :            :         literallen += 1;
   17013                 :            :         literalbuf[literallen] = '\0';
   17014                 :            : }
   17015                 :            : 
   17016                 :            : 
   17017                 :            : /*
   17018                 :            :  * One might be tempted to write pstrdup(literalbuf) instead of this,
   17019                 :            :  * but for long literals this is much faster because the length is
   17020                 :            :  * already known.
   17021                 :            :  */
   17022                 :            : static char *
   17023                 :            : litbufdup(void)
   17024                 :            : {
   17025                 :            :         char *new;
   17026                 :            : 
   17027                 :            :         new = palloc(literallen + 1);
   17028                 :            :         memcpy(new, literalbuf, literallen+1);
   17029                 :            :         return new;
   17030                 :            : }

   17032                 :            : static int
   17033                 :            : hexval(unsigned char c)
   17034                 :            : {
   17035                 :            :         if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
   17036                 :            :                 return c - '0';
   17037                 :            :         if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
   17038                 :            :                 return c - 'a' + 0xA;
   17039                 :            :         if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
   17040                 :            :                 return c - 'A' + 0xA;

   17042                 :            :         return 0; /* not reached */
   17043                 :            : }

   17045                 :            : static void
   17046                 :            : check_unicode_value(pg_wchar c, char * loc)
   17047                 :            : {
   17048                 :            :         if (GetDatabaseEncoding() == PG_UTF8)
   17049                 :            :                 return;
   17050                 :            : 
   17051                 :            :         if (c > 0x7F)
   17052                 :            :         {
   17053                 :            :                 yylloc += (char *) loc - literalbuf + 3;   /* 3 for U&" */
   17054                 :            :                 yyerror("Unicode escape values cannot be used for code point values above 007F when the server encoding is not UTF8");
   17055                 :            :         }
   17056                 :            : }
   17057                 :            : 
   17058                 :            : static char *
   17059                 :            : litbuf_udeescape(unsigned char escape)
   17060                 :            : {
   17061                 :            :         char *new;
   17062                 :            :         char *in, *out;
   17063                 :            : 
   17064                 :            :         if (isxdigit(escape)
   17065                 :            :                 || escape == '+'
   17066                 :            :                 || escape == '\''
   17067                 :            :                 || escape == '"'
   17068                 :            :                 || scanner_isspace(escape))
   17069                 :            :         {
   17070                 :            :                 yylloc += literallen + base_yyleng + 1;
   17071                 :            :                 yyerror("invalid Unicode escape character");
   17072                 :            :         }
   17073                 :            : 
   17074                 :            :         /*
   17075                 :            :          * This relies on the subtle assumption that a UTF-8 expansion
   17076                 :            :          * cannot be longer than its escaped representation.
   17077                 :            :          */
   17078                 :            :         new = palloc(literallen + 1);
   17079                 :            : 
   17080                 :            :         in = literalbuf;
   17081                 :            :         out = new;
   17082                 :            :         while (*in)
   17083                 :            :         {
   17084                 :            :                 if (in[0] == escape)
   17085                 :            :                 {
   17086                 :            :                         if (in[1] == escape)
   17087                 :            :                         {
   17088                 :            :                                 *out++ = escape;
   17089                 :            :                                 in += 2;
   17090                 :            :                         }
   17091                 :            :                         else if (isxdigit(in[1]) && isxdigit(in[2]) && isxdigit(in[3]) && isxdigit(in[4]))
   17092                 :            :                         {
   17093                 :            :                                 pg_wchar unicode = hexval(in[1]) * 16*16*16 + hexval(in[2]) * 16*16 + hexval(in[3]) * 16 + hexval(in[4]);
   17094                 :            :                                 check_unicode_value(unicode, in);
   17095                 :            :                                 unicode_to_utf8(unicode, (unsigned char *) out);
   17096                 :            :                                 in += 5;
   17097                 :            :                                 out += pg_mblen(out);
   17098                 :            :                         }
   17099                 :            :                         else if (in[1] == '+'
   17100                 :            :                                          && isxdigit(in[2]) && isxdigit(in[3])
   17101                 :            :                                          && isxdigit(in[4]) && isxdigit(in[5])
   17102                 :            :                                          && isxdigit(in[6]) && isxdigit(in[7]))
   17103                 :            :                         {
   17104                 :            :                                 pg_wchar unicode = hexval(in[2]) * 16*16*16*16*16 + hexval(in[3]) * 16*16*16*16 + hexval(in[4]) * 16*16*16
   17105                 :            :                                                                         + hexval(in[5]) * 16*16 + hexval(in[6]) * 16 + hexval(in[7]);
   17106                 :            :                                 check_unicode_value(unicode, in);
   17107                 :            :                                 unicode_to_utf8(unicode, (unsigned char *) out);
   17108                 :            :                                 in += 8;
   17109                 :            :                                 out += pg_mblen(out);
   17110                 :            :                         }
   17111                 :            :                         else
   17112                 :            :                         {
   17113                 :            :                                 yylloc += in - literalbuf + 3;   /* 3 for U&" */
   17114                 :            :                                 yyerror("invalid Unicode escape value");
   17115                 :            :                         }
   17116                 :            :                 }
   17117                 :            :                 else
   17118                 :            :                         *out++ = *in++;
   17119                 :            :         }
   17120                 :            : 
   17121                 :            :         *out = '\0';
   17122                 :            :         /*
   17123                 :            :          * We could skip pg_verifymbstr if we didn't process any non-7-bit-ASCII
   17124                 :            :          * codes; but it's probably not worth the trouble, since this isn't
   17125                 :            :          * likely to be a performance-critical path.
   17126                 :            :          */
   17127                 :            :         pg_verifymbstr(new, out - new, false);
   17128                 :            :         return new;
   17129                 :            : }

   17131                 :            : static unsigned char
   17132                 :            : unescape_single_char(unsigned char c)
   17133                 :            : {
   17134                 :            :         switch (c)
   17135                 :            :         {
   17136                 :            :                 case 'b':
   17137                 :            :                         return '\b';
   17138                 :            :                 case 'f':
   17139                 :            :                         return '\f';
   17140                 :            :                 case 'n':
   17141                 :            :                         return '\n';
   17142                 :            :                 case 'r':
   17143                 :            :                         return '\r';
   17144                 :            :                 case 't':
   17145                 :            :                         return '\t';
   17146                 :            :                 default:
   17147                 :            :                         /* check for backslash followed by non-7-bit-ASCII */
   17148                 :            :                         if (c == '\0' || IS_HIGHBIT_SET(c))
   17149                 :            :                                 saw_non_ascii = true;
   17150                 :            : 
   17151                 :            :                         return c;
   17152                 :            :         }
   17153                 :            : }

   17155                 :            : static void
   17156                 :            : addunicode(pg_wchar c)
   17157                 :            : {
   17158                 :            :         char buf[8];
   17159                 :            : 
   17160                 :            :         if (c == 0 || c > 0x10FFFF)
   17161                 :            :                 yyerror("invalid Unicode escape value");
   17162                 :            :         if (c > 0x7F)
   17163                 :            :         {
   17164                 :            :                 if (GetDatabaseEncoding() != PG_UTF8)
   17165                 :            :                         yyerror("Unicode escape values cannot be used for code point values above 007F when the server encoding is not UTF8");
   17166                 :            :                 saw_non_ascii = true;
   17167                 :            :         }
   17168                 :            :         unicode_to_utf8(c, (unsigned char *)buf);
   17169                 :            :         addlit(buf, pg_mblen(buf));
   17170                 :            : }

   17172                 :            : static void
   17173                 :            : check_string_escape_warning(unsigned char ychar)
   17174                 :            : {
   17175                 :            :         if (ychar == '\'')
   17176                 :            :         {
   17177                 :            :                 if (warn_on_first_escape && escape_string_warning)
   17178                 :            :                         ereport(WARNING,
   17179                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_NONSTANDARD_USE_OF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER),
   17180                 :            :                                          errmsg("nonstandard use of \\' in a string literal"),
   17181                 :            :                                          errhint("Use '' to write quotes in strings, or use the escape string syntax (E'...')."),
   17182                 :            :                                          lexer_errposition()));
   17183                 :            :                 warn_on_first_escape = false;   /* warn only once per string */
   17184                 :            :         }
   17185                 :            :         else if (ychar == '\\')
   17186                 :            :         {
   17187                 :            :                 if (warn_on_first_escape && escape_string_warning)
   17188                 :            :                         ereport(WARNING,
   17189                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_NONSTANDARD_USE_OF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER),
   17190                 :            :                                          errmsg("nonstandard use of \\\\ in a string literal"),
   17191                 :            :                                          errhint("Use the escape string syntax for backslashes, e.g., E'\\\\'."),
   17192                 :            :                                          lexer_errposition()));
   17193                 :            :                 warn_on_first_escape = false;   /* warn only once per string */
   17194                 :            :         }
   17195                 :            :         else
   17196                 :            :                 check_escape_warning();
   17197                 :            : }
   17198                 :            : 
   17199                 :            : static void
   17200                 :            : check_escape_warning(void)
   17201                 :            : {
   17202                 :            :         if (warn_on_first_escape && escape_string_warning)
   17203                 :            :                 ereport(WARNING,
   17204                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_NONSTANDARD_USE_OF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER),
   17205                 :            :                                  errmsg("nonstandard use of escape in a string literal"),
   17206                 :            :                                  errhint("Use the escape string syntax for escapes, e.g., E'\\r\\n'."),
   17207                 :            :                                  lexer_errposition()));
   17208                 :            :         warn_on_first_escape = false;   /* warn only once per string */
   17209                 :            : }
